rules.description: Produce natural-sounding translation that captures intended meaning and transfers it into natural language suited to a wide Brazilian Portuguese-speaking audience.
rules.notes: Main goal is to produce most natural-sounding translation

rules.description: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax; sentences need to be rewritten with adapted syntax.
rules.notes: Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language

rules.description: Use clear and precise style that is easily understood and does not read like a translation.
Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicism. Also avoid enriching the text excessively.
Buttons should typically be translated as verbs, generally using the infinitive form. Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions.
rules.notes: Pay attention to possible character/space limitations

rules.description: Use infinitive form for button translation without articles.
Use infinitive form for compound button actions.

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions, including terminology lists before translating product, application, or feature names as many are trademarked.
Replace English examples with Portuguese equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation.
rules.notes: Example given for wildcard operators explanation

rules.description: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural. Rephrase unclear sentences and rework sentences that sound like translation.
rules.notes: Break up long English sentences into smaller friendlier parts

rules.description: Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations.
Use clear and coherent style that target reader should understand easily.
Be objective: neutral, do not insert own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct.
Content and numbers should be free of mistakes.
Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so that text does not sound like a translation.
Do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs).
Keep an eye on the target reader: use an appropriate tone.
For internal cross-references, translate references to chapters, sections, headings or titles within the file or document being translated.
For external cross-references, check if text or document has been localized before and match relevant translations.
rules.notes: If document not translated before, leave in source language

rules.description: Cross-references shall be referenced consistently throughout translation and all files.
Do not overuse 'Por favor/Favor'; be more direct and use imperative form or similar constructions where appropriate.
rules.example_incorrect: Por favor, insira seu nome de usuário.
rules.notes: Brazilian Portuguese does not often use equivalent of 'Please'

rules.description: Brazilian Portuguese usually gives priority to masculine forms, unless differently needed based on specific context.
rules.example_correct: A equipe de serviço entrará em contato com o cliente e lhe fornecerá informações.
rules.example_incorrect: A equipe de serviço entrará em contato com a/o cliente e lhe fornecerá informações.

rules.description: Avoid passive voice whenever possible; use active form to improve readability.
rules.example_correct: O software salva todos os arquivos no diretório.
rules.example_incorrect: Todos os arquivos são salvos no diretório pelo software.
rules.notes: Passive voice only advised in legal and official documents

rules.description: Use active voice for better readability in general texts.
rules.example_correct: Este informe destaca alguns novos recursos.
rules.example_incorrect: Alguns novos recursos estão destacados neste informe.

rules.description: Do not transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to target; simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives.
rules.example_correct: Você pode editar os arquivos em um computador ou dispositivo móvel.
rules.example_incorrect: Você pode editar seus arquivos no seu computador ou no seu dispositivo móvel.
rules.notes: Sometimes 'our' refers to a subject in third person

rules.description: For proper use of articles, refer to standard Brazilian Portuguese grammar rules.
Translate -ing form based on context using most appropriate option: verb, noun, infinitive clause, subordinate clause, or gerund.
rules.example_correct: Nossos controladores acompanham um cache que protege a integridade dos dados
rules.notes: Multiple translation options available depending on context

rules.description: Use appropriate translation for -ing form in implementation contexts.
rules.example_correct: Esses serviços podem auxiliar as implementações, desde a arquitetura do site inicial até a implementação de um farm completo.

rules.description: Use gerund form for 'by + -ing' constructions.
rules.example_correct: Acessando este site, você aceita as condições gerais (...)

rules.description: Check context, concentrate on meaning and render as clearly as possible; avoid additional unnecessary words.
rules.example_correct: Nome, endereço de email e telefone
rules.example_incorrect: Seu nome, seu endereço de email, seu telefone

rules.description: Eliminate redundant expressions for better conciseness.
rules.example_incorrect: Isso significa que agora, você pode...

rules.description: Use pronouns or other methods to avoid repetitions which are acceptable in English but not in Brazilian Portuguese.
rules.example_correct: Para excluir um arquivo do computador, selecione-o no painel direito e clique em Excluir.
rules.example_incorrect: Para excluir um arquivo do computador, selecione o arquivo no painel direito e clique em Excluir.

rules.description: Be careful with false friends and literal translations that can have different meaning from source.
rules.notes: For English 'actually'

rules.description: Use correct translation for 'support'.
Use correct translation for 'comprehensive'.
Use correct translation for 'particular'.
Use correct translation for 'proper'.
Use correct translation for 'pretend'.
Use capital letter for proper names, product names, geographical names.
Use capital letter for first word after period, exclamation mark, question mark, ellipsis.
In title case, capitalize nouns and adjectives of organizations, bodies, departments, awards, and events.
Use lower case for nationalities, languages, religions, days of the week, months, season names, currencies, job titles.
Use lower case for months.
Use lower case for currencies.
Use lower case for languages.
Use lower case for nationalities.
In titles, headings, UI options, only the first word of the string should be capitalized.
In page layout titles, only capitalize first word.
In model names, only capitalize first word.
In legal texts, retain capitalization that conveys specific meaning.
rules.example_correct: O presente acordo (o "Acordo") é um acordo compulsório
rules.example_incorrect: O presente acordo (o "acordo") é um acordo compulsório
rules.notes: Exception for legal texts where capitalization has specific meaning

rules.description: Preserve capitalization in software references in legal contexts.
rules.example_correct: Instale e use uma cópia do Software.
rules.example_incorrect: Instale e use uma cópia do software.

rules.description: Preserve all upper case formatting when whole paragraphs are written that way as it may have legal implications.
Run spell-checker when translation is completed.
Run Xbench report using updated glossaries and export results to Excel spreadsheet with comments.
rules.notes: Add 'FP' (false positive) or 'Fixed' in column G

rules.description: Brand name tags need to be separated from preceding or following text with a space.
Use non-breaking space before double punctuation signs: colons, semicolons, question marks, exclamation marks and quotation marks.
rules.notes: Press Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or Alt+0160

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions from being separated at end of line (people's names, numbers, software names and versions).
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces.
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: No space is needed between a word and a round bracket.
Insert comma before 'e' only when absolutely required at end of enumeration to avoid ambiguity.
Use comma before 'e', 'ou' or 'nem' if coordinated elements have different subjects.
No comma needed when coordinates are simple.
Generally insert comma between elements coordinated by conjunction other than 'e', 'ou' or 'nem'.
Do not add comma before subordinate clauses (common CAT tool mistake).
rules.example_incorrect: O pessoal de serviço informou, que um caso será gerado para o problema informado por telefone.
rules.notes: Typical mistake originated by some CAT tools

rules.description: Use period to end sentence, paragraph or abbreviated word.
When sentence is placed between brackets or quotes, place period outside.
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in table, or after captions unless expression is a full sentence.
Do not use full stops if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon.
Try to avoid using colons; prefer commas or periods wherever possible, or use expression to avoid colon use.
rules.example_correct: Abra o assistente Criar um módulo e clique na guia Pacote.
rules.example_incorrect: Abra o assistente Criar um módulo: clique na guia Pacote.
rules.notes: English makes wider use of colons

rules.description: Colon must be preceded by non-breaking space.
Use semi-colons mainly to separate items in enumeration when using only commas would be confusing.
rules.notes: Semi-colons used less in Brazilian Portuguese than English

rules.description: Avoid use of semi-colons; start new sentence instead.
Semi-colon must be preceded by non-breaking space.
Replace semi-colon with comma or new sentence for better readability.
rules.example_correct: As categorias incluem: coomputadores, laptops e dispositivos móveis, sistemas operacionais, aplicativos e aplicativos móveis, bem como rede, roteadores e firewalls.
rules.example_incorrect: As categorias incluem: coomputadores, laptops e dispositivos móveis; sistemas operacionais, aplicativos e aplicativos móveis; rede, roteadores e firewalls.

rules.description: Replace semi-colon between related sentences with period for better flow.
rules.example_correct: Observe as informações nos campos Nome de usuário e Senha. Você precisará delas mais tarde para fazer login.
rules.example_incorrect: Observe as informações nos campos Nome de usuário e Senha; você precisará delas mais tarde para fazer login.

rules.description: Avoid question marks as they are much less used in Brazilian Portuguese than English.
Question mark must be preceded by non-breaking space.
Replace question format with statement format when appropriate.
Exclamation marks not as common in Brazilian Portuguese as in English; sometimes better to remove them.
When exclamation marks appear in groups, keep only one.
Exclamation mark must be preceded by non-breaking space.
Use en dash as minus sign.
Use en dash where it replaces colon or comma, but prefer colon.
Use proper format for indicating time ranges.
rules.example_correct: De segunda a sexta, das 8 h às 16 h

rules.description: Use proper format for page ranges.
Use colon rather than em dash for instructions.
rules.example_correct: Ativar: clique nessa opção para ativar os servidor.
rules.example_incorrect: Ativar — Clique nessa opção para ativar o servidor.

rules.description: Use round brackets for further explanations that would disrupt sentence, for spelling out acronyms, for indicating singular/plural forms or for translations of UI items when UI not localized.
rules.example_correct: Indique o endereço (UNC ou URL) e clique em Validar.

rules.description: Use round brackets for spelling out acronyms.
rules.example_correct: Arquivos grandes podem ser transferidos via FTP (File Transfer Protocol).

rules.description: Use round brackets for UI translations when UI not localized.
Use square brackets for indicating variables or additional information within round brackets.
Always use double straight quotation marks consistently, regardless of quotation mark type used in source text.
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207. NEVER use straight single quote for variables to avoid functional issues

rules.description: Use straight apostrophe consistently instead of curly apostrophe.

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are complete sentences with conjugated verb, begin with capital and end with period.
rules.notes: Example 1 – complete sentences

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If bulleted items are phrases or single words, items shall not be followed by period.
rules.notes: Example 2 – phrases/words

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If list items are not full sentences and depend syntactically on introductory sentence, start with lower case letter and end with semicolon. End last item with period.
rules.notes: Example 3 – continued sentences

rules.description: Avoid abbreviations whenever possible, but may abbreviate words in UI options.
Shorten word by at least two letters, abbreviation should end with consonant and end with period.
If word has double consonant, abbreviate after second consonant.
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project-related requirements and should always be investigated to confirm localizability.
If there is existing translation, insert it before English term.
rules.notes: When acronym appears before spelled out version

rules.description: If there is no translation, keep spelled out term between brackets.
If there is translation, insert developed version and acronym between brackets.
rules.notes: When spelled out term is followed by acronym in brackets

rules.description: When acronym is not spelled out, use acronym only.
Write numbers in full when used as nouns.
Write numbers in full when at beginning of sentence.
Write numbers in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually numbers up to 9.
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'.
Write dates, large numbers, ages, document references as numbers.
Replace decimal point with comma and thousand separator with dot.
Use proper thousand separator formatting.
Use proper formatting for large numbers without decimals.
Use proper formatting for millions.
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms.
Units of measure should be separated from preceding number by non-breaking space.
Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent exception to metric conversion.
Percentages are usually written in figures followed by % symbol with non-breaking space between figure and symbol.
Adapt date and time formats to Brazilian Portuguese standards with day-month format, not month-day.
Short date format should be DD/MM/YYYY.
rules.notes: Can be separated by slash or less frequently hyphen

rules.description: Long date format should be dddd d MMMM yyyy.
Standard time format is hh:mm:ss using 24-hour clock format.
Use proper time range format.
Use proper date format for month-year combinations.
Convert ISO date format to Brazilian standard.
For Reais, use currency symbol and non-breaking space.
For US dollars and other currencies, use currency symbol and non-breaking space.
Ellipsis indicates omitted words in direct quote, not trailing thoughts.
Don't capitalize first word after ellipsis unless it's proper noun.
Don't begin sentence with ellipsis.
Don't use spaces before or after ellipsis.
If ending complete sentence with ellipsis, no period is needed.
Ellipsis is one character, not three periods. In plain-text format, use three periods.
rules.example_correct: Four score and seven years... a new nation.
rules.notes: On Mac: opt + [ ; ], Windows: alt + 0133

rules.description: Translate 'about' as 'sobre'.
Translate 'as shown in figure xx' as 'como mostra a imagem xx'.
Translate 'For more information on xxx, see xxx' as 'Para obter mais informações sobre xxx, consulte xxx'.
rules.example_correct: Para obter mais informações sobre xxx, consulte xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'N/A (Non Available)' as 'N/D (não disponível)'.
Translate 'Please note that' as 'Observe que'.
Translate 'Refer to xxx for instructions on xxx' as 'Consulte xxx para obter instruções sobre xxx'.
rules.example_correct: Consulte xxx para obter instruções sobre xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'registered trademark' as 'marca registrada'.
Translate 'troubleshooting' as 'solução de problemas'.
Translate 'trademark' as 'marca comercial'.
Translate 'User's Guide' as 'Guia do usuário'.
Translate 'want (to), would like' as 'deseja'.
Translate 'we recommend…' as 'convém...'.
Translate 'Follow these steps to xxx' as 'Para xxx, siga estas etapas:'.
Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on first mention in translated content unless otherwise indicated.

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Complete extensive research to determine whether official, approved trademarked translated version is available before using as in source.

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: For third party product names, follow localized equivalence in official websites if available.
Use registered trademark symbol on first mention only unless specifically instructed otherwise.
Always apply superscript formatting to registered trademark symbol.
rules.notes: Mac: opt + [ r ], Windows: alt + 0174

rules.description: When registered trademark symbol needed in plain-text format, use lowercase 'r' in parentheses.
Use service mark symbol on first mention only unless specifically instructed otherwise.
When service mark symbol needed in plain-text format, use lowercase 'sm' in parentheses.
Use trademark symbol on first mention only unless specifically instructed otherwise.
When trademark symbol needed in plain-text format, use lowercase 'tm' in parentheses.
rules.notes: Mac: opt + [ 2 ], Windows: alt + 0153

rules.description: Query if URLs have local version and modify document accordingly; if not, leave URL as it appears in source English text.
If software has been translated, match relevant translation memory or string list provided with translation package.
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format with English first, followed by target in brackets.
Translate country and city names based on your country/region's conventions.
Localize address formats based on your country/region's conventions if necessary.
If source only includes toll free numbers and US numbers, add international country code to local number for international users.
File names should include KID or JIRA number, name and asset type, your initials, date, followed by version number.
